name: Wendy Adams - Structure Deck Series ( Core Set )
description_md: Hello Fellow investigators Welcome to my structure deck series! ## Concept Since the game contains a large pool of cards and this might seem a bit confusing for newer players I decided to create some deck ideas of old investigators in the game. With the new product of starter decks coming on the way, it gave me a reason to create some decklists through every cycle. I 'm going to give you an upgrade list for everyone through every cycle. We continue with our Survivor Wendy Adams from the core set and I'll progress her deck through Dunwich, Carcosa, etc.. Let start with the show, shall we? ## Wendy Adams Pros and Cons Despite being a little kid Wendy is a beast. She is the tank of your core set in your multiplayer games. She is our Lita Chantler leaving her alone with the monsters sacrificing her for a greater cause! :D She has average health and sanity (7) and a good and skills (4). Where she shines is her ability wherein a crucial test it can turn the tides of victory and defeat. On top of that, her signature card [Wendy's Amulet](/card/01014) comes to turn the downside of your ability to a bonus. Not only that it gives you the chance to play your powerful event cards for a second time so you better plan ahead with your gameplay strategy. You soon realize that Wendy has a complex deck depending on two-card combos. If you don't draw specific cards early on you 'll found yourself in a difficult spot. That's why our deck is based on card drawing in order to get the essential components needed. Another disadvantage is our fighting skill (1) which makes it difficult to kill monsters, at least for now with the current pool we have. Ηer weakness [Abandoned and Alone](/card/01015) it can be pretty nasty in the middle game while you stockpile your items for your [Scavenging](/card/01073) and your events for [Wendy's Amulet](/card/01014). The most important thing is to carefully plan ahead your actions and quickly place your key cards into play in order to make your deck fully functional. ## Gameplay I need to mention that in your multiplayer games if your fellow investigator is Skids let him play with [Leo De Luca](/card/01048). You swap Leo with a second [Rabbit's Foot](/card/01075) and a [Stray Cat](/card/01076) until you upgrade these cards to 2x [Cat Burglar](/card/01055). Try to get your [Wendy's Amulet](/card/01014) and a [Flashlight](/card/01087) in your hand. Mulligan hard if you have to. Keep at least an event in your hand, ideally ["Look what I found!"](/card/01079) in order to combo the two cards. Also ["Look what I found!"](/card/01079) combos well with [Rabbit's Foot](/card/01075). Use [Flashlight](/card/01087) in low shroud locations and try to keep ["Look what I found!"](/card/01079) for the high ones. [Scavenging](/card/01073) can help you recover your [Baseball Bat](/card/01074), [Knife](/card/01086), [Flashlight](/card/01087) even your [Wendy's Amulet](/card/01014). While it's not that good with the current card pool it escalates later on the other cycles. While you have a good you need [Guts](/card/01089) to avoid specific treachery that will cost you an asset (can't tell which one!). [Leo De Luca](/card/01048) is great.. in his upgrade version. While an extra action is essential his cost is too high in the first scenario and takes away resources from [Hard Knocks](/card/01049). Be careful and add him to your field when you 're confident enough or you haven't played [Hard Knocks](/card/01049) yet. [Lucky!](/card/01080) and [Emergency Cache](/card/01088) is self-explanatory and try to combo them with [Wendy's Amulet](/card/01014) for a maximum profit. Ex: Play [Emergency Cache](/card/01088) play [Wendy's Amulet](/card/01014) [Emergency Cache](/card/01088) for 6 resources. Now your [Hard Knocks](/card/01049) becomes pretty viable with [Baseball Bat](/card/01074) in your hands. Last tip for [Knife](/card/01086): better use it for low life enemies that pokes your life like rats. Ideal First Hand: [Wendy's Amulet](/card/01014), [Flashlight](/card/01087), [Emergency Cache](/card/01088), [Leo De Luca](/card/01048), [Knife](/card/01086) ## Upgrade Route Again there are two routes in the core set. The multiplayer tank and Leo solo/multi playthrough. The Multiplayer tank: Your core cards on this build are 2x [Aquinnah](/card/01082), [Elder Sign Amulet](/card/01095). Try to get before the 3rd scenario 2x [Will to Survive](/card/01085) and if you have some XP left 2x [Lucky!](/card/01084). Leo Solo/multi: Go for 2x [Leo De Luca](/card/01054) and 2x [Lucky!](/card/01084) unless you have a Skids co-player so go for 2x [Cat Burglar](/card/01055). After that go for 2x [Will to Survive](/card/01085) and a [Elder Sign Amulet](/card/01095) if you have XP left. This route its the one I used the most. ## Summary Wendy is pretty fun to play! You can either choose to avoid fighting monsters or in a moment find yourself engaged with 3 monsters just to save your fellow investigators and throw to you a [Dynamite Blast](/card/01024) sacrificing yourself to the Old Ones :D Her deck might have some complexity but when you used to it can be quite fun for you. I say go for it, try her combos and be creative! I hope you like this guide and we'll continue with her Dunwich deck build. To be continued..
